zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Elizabeth Cheshire於1230年出生在英國爾盧可時，她的父親Richard de Cheshire Kingsley65歲，母親Lady Joan de Stourton40歲。她於大約1246年與Sir John Browne I.在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯坦福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1317年在英國英格蘭爾武特金頓享年88歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Elizabeth Cheshire于1230年出生在英国爾盧可时，她的父亲Richard de Cheshire Kingsley65岁，母亲Lady Joan de Stourton40岁。她于大约1246年与Sir John Browne I.在英国英格兰林可爾史爾斯坦福结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1317年在英国英格兰爾武特金頓享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cheshire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name for someone from the county of Cheshire in northwestern England, the name of which is early recorded as Cestrescire, from the name of the county seat, Chester , + Old English scīr ‘district, division’.

variants.name: Chester
meanings: English: habitational name from Chester, the county seat of Cheshire, or from any of various smaller places called with this word (as for example Little Chester in Derbyshire, Chester le Street in County Durham, or Chesters in Northumberland), which is from Old English ceaster ‘Roman fort or walled city’ (from Latin castra ‘legionary camp’).

variants.name: Chesser
meanings: English (Middlesex) and Scottish (Aberdeenshire, Midlothian): variant of   Cheshire  .

variants.name: Cheshier
meanings: English: variant of   Cheshire  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Chessher
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Cheshire  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Elizabeth Cheshire was born in 1230, in Runcorn, Cheshire, England, her father, Richard de Cheshire Kingsley, was 65 and her mother, Lady Joan de Stourton, was 40. She married Sir John Browne I. about 1246, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1317, in Utkinton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Elizabeth Cheshire est née en 1230, à Runcorn, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Cheshire Kingsley, avait 65 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan de Stourton, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Browne I. environ 1246, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1317, à Utkinton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 88 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Elizabeth Cheshire 1230 in Runcorn, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Cheshire Kingsley 65 und ihre Mutter Lady Joan de Stourton 40. Sie hat um 1246 in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Browne I. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1317 im Alter von 88 Jahren in Utkinton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Elizabeth Cheshire è nata il nel 1230 nel Runcorn, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Richard de Cheshire Kingsley, aveva 65 anni e sua madre, Lady Joan de Stourton, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Browne I. circa 1246, nel Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1317, nel Utkinton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 88 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Elizabeth Cheshireが1230年にイギリスチェシュアイアルンコルンで生まれた時，父Richard de Cheshire Kingsleyは65歳，母Lady Joan de Stourtonは40歳であった。約1246年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードでSir John Browne I.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1317年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアウトキントンで88歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Elizabeth Cheshire(이)가 1230년 영국 체실르 런코른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Cheshire Kingsley(은)는 65세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan de Stourton(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 대략 1246년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 Sir John Browne I.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1317년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 우트킨턴에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Elizabeth Cheshire nasceu em 1230, em Runcorn, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Cheshire Kingsley, tinha 65 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joan de Stourton, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Browne I. aproximadamente 1246, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1317, em Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 88 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Elizabeth Cheshire родилась в 1230 г. в местности рункорн, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Cheshire Kingsley было 65 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan de Stourton, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Browne I. около 1246 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1317 г. в местности уткинтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 88 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Elizabeth Cheshire nació en 1230, en Runcorn, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Cheshire Kingsley, tenía 65 años y su mamá, Lady Joan de Stourton, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Sir John Browne I. aproximadamente 1246, en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1317, en Utkinton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 88 años.
